All right, welcome to this final lesson of module two. This is where we are going to be coaching the third meta state, which is connecting to expanded possibility or their actualized vision. So what are we talking about with this? This is the destination. It's what invites resourcefulness in the form of power and courage to actually drive forward to this place, right, to this utopia, to this final destination, where everything they want lies and exists. It's their new reality. So it's not just desire, desire, which includes longing, fear and fear of unrealization, which we're going to talk all about in the next module in unpacking the situation on just all. Instead, this is their connection, their link to the future version of them who has what they want, and is experiencing the fullness, the full dimensionality of that possibility and what it represents, what it means, what it enables, what it empowers, what new opportunities it unlocks. So all this exists in this future version of them. And we need them to be connected or at least linked to this future vision of them, future version of them, if we're going to have a successful enrollment call. So if they don't have at least a degree of belief in this possibility and a sense of inevitability or destiny, the doubt they feel, the fear they have to overcome will all be quite difficult, right? There's not enough driving force pulling them along that path, pulling them through the whole resolution of this situational gestalt and all the challenges within it that we're going to talk about in the next module. So many people won't feel safe in embracing the possibility of them having what they want. And in doing so, they distance and tend to dissociate from that reality, rendering it more of an abstract idea versus something they're actually actualizing or implicated in, right? So it tends to be more of an idea of me as a successful entrepreneur or me and my partner in a happy marriage or my kids getting into Harvard or whatever it looks like. It tends to be more of an idea, right, versus something that they're actually allowing themselves the full experience of implicating themselves within, which means that link between them now and this future version of them is not so solid, right? They feel less connected to this ethereal idea that they just haven't yet had the courage to claim. So at the beginning, their connection to that expanded sense of possibility can be murky, it could be fleeting, it could be loose, it could be filled with doubt, which will end up giving us stuff to work with while unpacking the situational gestalt in the next module, but it must at least be somewhat established early and reinforced often. So to establish this connection, it's really simple. We're looking for an acknowledgement that there is indeed a version of them that gets what they want, acknowledging, knowing that it might be difficult, but at least accepting that it's possible and that you're both dedicated to nurturing that sense of possibility throughout this column. That really is what nurturing is in a marketing context, where nurturing their shaky, flimsy hopes and turning them into an almost inevitable destiny. This is what it means to truly be of service, to truly nurture your prospects. It's all about reestablishing this connection to a new possibility and new vision, which until now, they only allowed themselves to experience as an abstract idea that they couldn't let themselves be implicated in, because it didn't feel like it was inevitable enough, it didn't feel possible enough. So this is what we're nurturing when we talk about nurturing. So how do we actually accomplish this practically? Well, we establish this connection with questioning, right? So putting all your doubts aside for a second, do you at least believe that it's within the realm of possibility that you're fully supporting yourself in your freelance writing career by December, right? And what would need to be true for that to happen? Right? So get them to explore. Is this even possible? Could I even get myself to believe and accept this possibility of me in this future outcome? In your heart, do you believe that it's possible for your relationship to transform? And if they say yes, what does it look and feel like in its transformed version, right? Get them to describe it the full dimensionality of it. And the next what has to be true for that to be real, right? And this is how you connect someone to a sense of possibility. Another one is there a version of you that lost those 35 pounds and feels energetic and alive. What do you think went right for them, right? Get them connected, get them in relationship to this future version of themselves. And next to expand what they view, right? As this image entailing, right? What's included in this vision? What are all the dimensions of it? How will you know you've gotten what you wanted, right? It's such a simple but powerful question. Is there a moment that really encapsulates that for you? So most people will have this specific image or moment or vision magnetizing them kind of like this still frame from their future timeline that just represents and encapsulates the totality of that version of themselves that was successful. So ask them, right? Like how will you know you've gotten what you wanted? So these are questions you can ask to help someone establish and maintain their connection to this expanded sense of possibility and the actualized version of themselves. So this line of questioning invites acknowledgement of the potentiality of a positive outcome, which is needed for one to invest, right? No one wants to play a game that they can't win, right? So this acknowledges at least the potentiality of that outcome existing. And it invites a resourcefulness and a renewed energy to explore achieving what they now know is possible, right? People will invest energy, people will invite invest resources, if they feel like there is a game to be won, if they feel like what they want is even possible. So that's why it's so vital to connect someone to their sense of possibility early. So you use it early, but you could also use it as a state shifter throughout the call, if you sense that they've slipped into doubt and closure or are refusing to orient around what's possible. If they get too caught up in victimhood and despair and fear of it not being possible, you can use this as a state shifter. So a state shifter once again would be returning to some of these questions we asked here, right? Can I interrupt you for a second? Do you believe that it is at least possible you can be achieving this, right? Let them return back to the state where they feel that's possible. And this is what is going to keep someone driving forward on your sales call, channeling the resources they have and leading them towards an empowered decision. So that's what we got for connected to vision. These are the three metastates I encourage you to revisit them often and practice them on your sales call. So that's it for now. I'll meet you in the next module where we'll go deeper into the concept of unpacking the situational distalt. I'll see you there.
